1	# FFPA CuTeDSL Internal Package (`ffpa_attn.cute`)
2	
3	This package hosts the internal CuTeDSL implementation used by the public
4	FFPA entry points. End users should normally call `ffpa_attn_func` or
5	`ffpa_attn_varlen_func` from the top-level `ffpa_attn` namespace and select the
6	backend with `backend="cutedsl"` or `forward_backend="cutedsl"` /
7	`backward_backend="cutedsl"`.
8	
9	The package name is `ffpa_attn.cute`, but the public backend string remains
10	`"cutedsl"` and `CuTeDSLBackend` remains the public configuration class.
11	
12	[cutedsl]: https://github.com/NVIDIA/cutlass/tree/main/python/CuTeDSL
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Public entry points
17	
18	| Public API | Where it lives | How it reaches this package |
19	|---|---|---|
20	| `ffpa_attn.ffpa_attn_func` | `src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py` | Dense SDPA-layout wrapper. Opt in with `backend="cutedsl"`. |
21	| `ffpa_attn.ffpa_attn_varlen_func` | `src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py` | Packed-THD varlen wrapper. CuTeDSL is the only backend. |
22	
23	Both APIs route into this package through architecture-aware wrappers defined in
24	`__init__.py`. Dense and varlen paths share the same SM90 specialised kernels
25	when `head_dim <= 512` on Hopper and otherwise fall back to the generic SM80
26	Split-D implementation.
27	
28	---
29	
30	## Usage
31	
32	### 1. Dense path through `ffpa_attn_func`
33	
34	```python
35	import torch
36	from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_func
37	
38	B, H, N, D = 1, 32, 8192, 512
39	q = torch.randn(B, H, N, D, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda", requires_grad=True)
40	k = torch.randn_like(q)
41	v = torch.randn_like(q)
42	
43	out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v, is_causal=True, backend="cutedsl")
44	out.sum().backward()
45	```
46	
47	Dense tensors use SDPA layout `[B, H, N, D]`. The wrapper converts to the
48	CuTeDSL-native `[B, N, H, D]` layout internally, dispatches through
49	`ffpa_attn::_fwd_cute` / `ffpa_attn::_bwd_cute`, then converts outputs back.
50	
51	`meta.fallback()` only handles hardware and head-dim mismatches. Unsupported
52	features such as `attn_mask`, `dropout_p > 0`, unsupported dtypes, or invalid
53	head-dim layouts still raise immediately.
54	
55	### 2. Varlen path through `ffpa_attn_varlen_func`
56	
57	```python
58	import torch
59	from ffpa_attn import ffpa_attn_varlen_func
60	
61	lengths = [4096, 4096]
62	total_q = sum(lengths)
63	num_heads, head_dim = 32, 512
64	
65	q = torch.randn(total_q, num_heads, head_dim, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda", requires_grad=True)
66	k = torch.randn_like(q)
67	v = torch.randn_like(q)
68	cu_seqlens = torch.tensor([0, 4096, 8192], dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
69	
70	out, lse = ffpa_attn_varlen_func(
71	  q,
72	  k,
73	  v,
74	  cu_seqlens_q=cu_seqlens,
75	  cu_seqlens_k=cu_seqlens,
76	  max_seqlen_q=max(lengths),
77	  max_seqlen_k=max(lengths),
78	  causal=True,
79	  return_lse=True,
80	)
81	out.sum().backward()
82	```
83	
84	The varlen path consumes packed `[T, H, D]` tensors natively and dispatches
85	through the autograd-registered `ffpa_attn::_varlen_fwd_cute` /
86	`ffpa_attn::_varlen_bwd_cute` custom ops.
87	
88	### 3. Internal availability helpers
89	
90	```python
91	import torch
92	from ffpa_attn.cute import cute_forward_available, cute_backward_available
93	
94	device = torch.device("cuda", 0)
95	if cute_forward_available(device) and cute_backward_available(device):
96	  ...
97	```
98	
99	These helpers only check the device-level prerequisite `compute capability >= 8.0`.
100	Per-call head-dim, dtype, and unsupported-feature validation still happens in
101	`_require_cute_supported()`.
102	
103	---
104	
105	## Architecture
106	
107	```
108	ffpa_attn_func(backend='cutedsl')
109	  └── FFPAAttnFunc.apply
110	        forward  → _ffpa_attn_forward_cute  (__init__.py)
111	                    └── torch.ops.ffpa_attn._fwd_cute
112	                          └── _forward_impl_for_device(...)
113	                                ├── _ffpa_attn_forward_sm90
114	                                └── _ffpa_attn_forward_sm80
115	        backward → _ffpa_attn_backward_cute  (__init__.py)
116	                    └── torch.ops.ffpa_attn._bwd_cute
117	                          └── _backward_impl_for_device(...)
118	                                ├── _ffpa_attn_backward_sm90
119	                                └── _ffpa_attn_backward_sm80
120	
121	ffpa_attn_varlen_func(...)
122	  └── _ffpa_attn_varlen_cute  (__init__.py)
123	        └── _ffpa_attn_varlen_impl
124	              └── torch.ops.ffpa_attn._varlen_fwd_cute / _varlen_bwd_cute
125	```
126	
127	The routing rule is simple:
128	
129	- `major == 9` and symmetric `head_dim <= 512` uses the SM90 specialised path.
130	- Every other supported architecture (`SM80/SM89/SM100/SM103/SM120/...`) and
131	  every larger supported dense head-dim uses the SM80 generic Split-D path.
132	
133	---
134	
135	## File roles
136	
137	| File | Role |
138	|---|---|
139	| `__init__.py` | Dense/varlen entry shims, SDPA↔FA layout adapters, torch custom ops, fake registrations, autograd registration, `_require_cute_supported()`, `cute_{forward,backward}_available()`, `cute_max_supported_head_dim()`. |
140	| `_utils.py` | Shared constants, validation helpers, optional-int encoding, and fake-mode helpers. |
141	| `_ffpa_fwd_sm90.py` | SM90 forward entry and compile cache. |
142	| `_ffpa_bwd_sm90.py` | SM90 backward entry, preprocess wiring, and compile caches. |
143	| `_ffpa_fwd_sm80.py` | SM80/SM89 and generic fallback forward entry. |
144	| `_ffpa_bwd_sm80.py` | SM80/SM89 and generic fallback backward entry. |
145	| `_fwd_d512_sm90.py` | Hopper D512 forward kernel. |
146	| `_fwd_d384_sm90.py` | Hopper D384-aware forward kernel. |
147	| `_fwd_generic_sm90.py` | Hopper generic forward wrapper. |
148	| `_fwd_generic_sm80.py` | Ampere-style generic forward kernel and scheduling logic. |
149	| `_bwd_preprocess.py` | Shared backward preprocess kernel. |
150	| `_dkdv_d512_sm90.py` | Hopper D512 dK/dV kernel. |
151	| `_dkdv_d384_sm90.py` | Hopper D384 dK/dV kernel. |
152	| `_dkdv_generic_sm90.py` | Hopper generic dK/dV wrapper. |
153	| `_dkdv_generic_sm80.py` | Generic SM80 fallback dK/dV kernel. |
154	| `_dq_d512_sm90.py` | Hopper D512 dQ kernel. |
155	| `_dq_d384_sm90.py` | Hopper D384 dQ kernel. |
156	| `_dq_generic_sm90.py` | Hopper generic dQ wrapper. |
157	| `_dq_generic_sm80.py` | Generic SM80 fallback dQ kernel. |
158	| `utils/` | Shared scheduling, masking, softmax, pipeline, cache, and codegen helpers. |
159	
160	Kernel class names are implementation details. File paths and exported wrapper
161	functions are the stable internal contract for this package.
162	
163	---
164	
165	## Hard constraints
166	
167	| Constraint | Detail |
168	|---|---|
169	| **GPU** | Device-level gate is `sm >= 80`. SM90 gets specialised kernels; other supported archs use the SM80 fallback. |
170	| **Dense head dim** | `320 <= D <= cute_max_supported_head_dim()`. Today that ceiling is `1024` via the SM80 fallback. |
171	| **SM90 specialised range** | `320 <= D <= 512` with symmetric q/k/v head-dim. |
172	| **SM80 fallback divisibility** | Dense fallback requires `D % 32 == 0` in forward and symmetric q/k/v head-dim. |
173	| **Varlen** | Uses the same architecture routing; feature support is still more constrained than dense and should be validated through the wrapper. |
174	| **Dtype** | fp16 or bf16 only. |
175	| **attn_mask** | Not supported. |
176	| **Dropout** | `dropout_p == 0.0` only. |
177	| **Varlen extras** | `seqused_k`, `block_table`, `num_splits`, `window_size`, `alibi_slopes`, `softcap`, `score_mod`, `aux_tensors`, `sink`, `attention_mask`, and `block_mask` are rejected. |
178	| **GQA / MQA** | Supported when head-count divisibility rules are satisfied. |
179	
180	Any ineligible call surfaces `NotImplementedError`, `TypeError`, or
181	`ValueError`; there is no silent feature fallback inside this package.
182	
183	---
184	
185	## Blackwell / SM120 investigation note
186	
187	The current kernels are not made Blackwell-compatible by simply relaxing the
188	Python-side `sm == 90` checks to `sm >= 90`. A May 2026 experiment on
189	`NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition`, (compute capability 12.0) showed that the D512 path reaches CuTeDSL JIT after the local gates are relaxed, but then fails inside CUTLASS DSL's Hopper
190	warpgroup MMA implementation:
191	
192	```text
193	cutlass.cute.nvgpu.common.OpError: expects arch to be Arch.sm_90a, but got Arch.sm_120a
194	```
195	
196	Forcing `CUTE_DSL_ARCH=sm_90a` can compile the Hopper target, but it fails at
197	runtime on Blackwell with `cudaErrorNoKernelImageForDevice`. Temporarily
198	relaxing the installed CUTLASS DSL `warpgroup.MmaOp` arch check from
199	`Arch.sm_90a` to `>= Arch.sm_90a` only moves the failure to NVVM module
200	serialization for `sm_120` / `sm_120a`. In other words, the blocker is not just
201	an over-strict guard in this package.
202	
203	Future Blackwell work should add a real SM100+/SM120 CuTeDSL path instead of
204	trying to reuse the Hopper `warpgroup` path. CUTLASS provides a separate
205	Blackwell stack based on `cutlass.cute.nvgpu.tcgen05` and
206	`cutlass.utils.blackwell_helpers.make_trivial_tiled_mma`. That helper constructs
207	`tcgen05.MmaF16BF16Op`, whose signature includes `CtaGroup` and uses Blackwell
208	operand sources such as `tcgen05.OperandSource.SMEM` / `TMEM`. This differs from
209	the current Hopper helper, which constructs `warpgroup.MmaF16BF16Op` and only
210	accepts `Arch.sm_90a`.
211	
212	Useful references for a future port:
213	
214	- `cutlass/utils/blackwell_helpers.py` - Blackwell `make_trivial_tiled_mma` and
215	  TMEM/TMA helper utilities.
216	- `cutlass/cute/nvgpu/tcgen05/mma.py` - Blackwell MMA op definitions and arch
217	  admissibility checks.
218	- `examples/python/CuTeDSL/cute/blackwell/kernel/attention/mixed_input_fmha/`
219	  in the CUTLASS checkout - attention examples using `tcgen05`, `CtaGroup`, and
220	  TMEM-backed PV-style MMA.
221	
222	---
223	
224	## References
225	
226	- **Public API** — `ffpa_attn_func` / `ffpa_attn_varlen_func` docstrings in `src/ffpa_attn/ffpa_attn_interface.py`.
227	- **CuTeDSL** — https://github.com/NVIDIA/cutlass/tree/main/python/CuTeDSL
228

1	"""FFPA CuTeDSL forward pass scaffold for SM80/SM89 Split-D kernels.
2	
3	This module owns the SM80/SM89 orchestration surface. The public signature is
4	kept aligned with :func:`ffpa_attn.cute._ffpa_fwd_sm90._ffpa_attn_forward_sm90`
5	so the existing CuTeDSL custom ops can dispatch by architecture without adding
6	new torch op schemas.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import math
12	from typing import Callable, Optional, Tuple
13	
14	import torch
15	import cutlass.cute as cute
16	
17	from ._fwd_generic_sm80 import FFPAAttnFwdSm80SplitD, FFPAAttnSm80SplitDPersistQ
18	from ._utils import (
19	  SM80_FWD_TILE_M,
20	  SM80_FWD_TILE_N,
21	  SM80_FWD_NUM_STAGES,
22	  SM80_FWD_NUM_THREADS,
23	  SM80_FWD_SPLIT_D_CHUNK,
24	  SM80_FWD_USE_PERSIST_Q,
25	  is_fake_mode,
26	  maybe_contiguous,
27	  _call_with_tvm_ffi_current_stream,
28	  _pick_split_d_chunk,
29	  _resolve_causal_local_window,
30	  _unsupported_training_features,
31	  _validate_max_seqlen_for_cu_seqlens,
32	  _validate_qkv_common,
33	  _validate_sm80_arch,
34	  _validate_sm80_head_dims,
35	  _validate_tensor,
36	  _validate_training_dtype,
37	  torch2cute_dtype_map,
38	)
39	from .utils.cache_utils import get_jit_cache
40	from .utils import fa_logging
41	from .utils.cute_dsl_utils import to_cute_tensor
42	
43	
44	def _ffpa_attn_forward_sm80(
45	  q: torch.Tensor,
46	  k: torch.Tensor,
47	  v: torch.Tensor,
48	  cu_seqlens_q: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
49	  cu_seqlens_k: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
50	  max_seqlen_q: Optional[int] = None,
51	  max_seqlen_k: Optional[int] = None,
52	  softmax_scale: Optional[float] = None,
53	  causal: bool = False,
54	  softcap: Optional[float] = None,
55	  window_size_left: Optional[int] = None,
56	  window_size_right: Optional[int] = None,
57	  pack_gqa: Optional[bool] = None,
58	  score_mod: Optional[Callable] = None,
59	  mask_mod: Optional[Callable] = None,
60	  return_lse: bool = False,
61	  out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
62	  lse: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
63	  aux_tensors: Optional[list[torch.Tensor]] = None,
64	) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
65	  """SM80/SM89 Split-D forward launcher.
66	
67	  :param q: Query tensor in dense ``[B, N, H, D]`` or packed ``[T, H, D]`` layout.
68	  :param k: Key tensor with the same head dimension as ``q``.
69	  :param v: Value tensor with the same head dimension as ``q``.
70	  :param cu_seqlens_q: Optional packed-query sequence offsets.
71	  :param cu_seqlens_k: Optional packed-key sequence offsets.
72	  :param max_seqlen_q: Maximum query sequence length for varlen inputs.
73	  :param max_seqlen_k: Maximum key sequence length for varlen inputs.
74	  :param softmax_scale: Attention scale. Resolved by the caller when ``None``.
75	  :param causal: Whether lower-right causal masking is applied.
76	  :param softcap: Unsupported for the SM80 Split-D path.
77	  :param window_size_left: Unsupported local-attention left window.
78	  :param window_size_right: Unsupported local-attention right window.
79	  :param pack_gqa: Whether query heads are packed for GQA/MQA.
80	  :param score_mod: Unsupported score modifier.
81	  :param mask_mod: Unsupported mask modifier.
82	  :param return_lse: Whether the caller needs LSE returned.
83	  :param out: Optional preallocated output tensor.
84	  :param lse: Optional preallocated LSE tensor.
85	  :param aux_tensors: Unsupported auxiliary tensors.
86	  :returns: ``(out, lse)`` matching the SM90 launcher contract.
87	  """
88	  # This SM80 forward launcher handles varlen natively (no per-segment
89	  # Python fallback) because the kernel itself is built on the same
90	  # TileScheduler + SeqlenInfoQK abstraction as the SM90 forward path.
91	  # The SM80 backward kernels (dK/dV, dQ) lack this infrastructure and
92	  # therefore rely on per-segment dense calls in their launcher.
93	  q, k, v = [maybe_contiguous(t) for t in (q, k, v)]
94	  (
95	    batch_size,
96	    seqlen_q,
97	    total_q,
98	    seqlen_k,
99	    num_head,
100	    num_head_kv,
101	    head_dim,
102	    head_dim_v,
103	  ) = _validate_qkv_common(
104	    q,
105	    k,
106	    v,
107	    cu_seqlens_q=cu_seqlens_q,
108	    cu_seqlens_k=cu_seqlens_k,
109	    validate_head_dims=_validate_sm80_head_dims,
110	  )
111	
112	  requires_grad = q.requires_grad or k.requires_grad or v.requires_grad
113	  _validate_training_dtype(q, k, v, requires_grad)
114	  _validate_max_seqlen_for_cu_seqlens(
115	    cu_seqlens_q, "cu_seqlens_q", max_seqlen_q, "max_seqlen_q"
116	  )
117	  _validate_max_seqlen_for_cu_seqlens(
118	    cu_seqlens_k, "cu_seqlens_k", max_seqlen_k, "max_seqlen_k"
119	  )
120	
121	  device_arch, cute_arch_key = _validate_sm80_arch()

260	      ffpa_fwd,
261	      q_tensor,
262	      k_tensor,
263	      v_tensor,

265	      lse_tensor,
266	      softmax_scale,

270	      window_size_right,
271	      cute_aux_tensors,
272	      current_stream,
273	    ]

275	      *compile_args,

277	        "--enable-tvm-ffi --ptxas-options '--verbose --warn-on-spills --warn-on-local-memory-usage'"
278	      ),

65	def _check_supported_options(
66	  *,
67	  source: str,
68	  dropout_p: float = 0.0,
69	  window_size: object = None,
70	  sink: torch.Tensor | None = None,
71	  attention_mask: torch.Tensor | None = None,
72	  block_mask: object | None = None,
73	  softcap: float | None = None,
74	  score_mod: object | None = None,
75	  aux_tensors: list[torch.Tensor] | None = None,
76	  seqused_k: torch.Tensor | None = None,
77	  block_table: torch.Tensor | None = None,
78	  num_splits: int | None = None,
79	  alibi_slopes: torch.Tensor | None = None,
80	) -> None:
81	  """Raise ``NotImplementedError`` for any non-default cutedsl-unsupported option.
82	
83	  The cutedsl SplitD kernels (``_ffpa_attn_varlen_impl``,
84	  ``_ffpa_attn_forward_cute``, ``_ffpa_attn_backward_cute``) only
85	  honor dense / varlen attention with optional causal masking.
86	  Every other option commonly exposed by attention APIs (mask tensors,
87	  sliding window, softcap, score_mod, aux tensors, FlashAttention varlen
88	  extensions, dropout) has no kernel-side implementation and is rejected
89	  up front so callers see one actionable error rather than a deep kernel
90	  crash or silent semantic divergence.
91	
92	  ``source`` is embedded in the error so the caller can tell which
93	  public-API surface produced the message.
94	  """
95	  unsupported: list[str] = []
96	  if dropout_p not in (None, 0.0):
97	    unsupported.append("dropout_p")
98	  if window_size is not None and window_size != (None, None):
99	    unsupported.append("window_size")
100	  if sink is not None:
101	    unsupported.append("sink")
102	  if attention_mask is not None:
103	    unsupported.append("attention_mask")
104	  if block_mask is not None:
105	    unsupported.append("block_mask")
106	  if softcap not in (None, 0.0):
107	    unsupported.append("softcap")
108	  if score_mod is not None:
109	    unsupported.append("score_mod")
110	  if aux_tensors is not None:
111	    unsupported.append("aux_tensors")
112	  if seqused_k is not None:
113	    unsupported.append("seqused_k")
114	  if block_table is not None:
115	    unsupported.append("block_table")
116	  if num_splits is not None:
117	    unsupported.append("num_splits")
118	  if alibi_slopes is not None:
119	    unsupported.append("alibi_slopes")
120	  if unsupported:
121	    raise NotImplementedError(
122	      f"{source} only supports dense/varlen attention with optional "
123	      f"causal masking; unsupported options: {', '.join(unsupported)}. "
124	      f"Use forward_backend='triton' when these options are required."
125	    )

128	def cute_forward_available(device: Optional[torch.device] = None) -> bool:
129	  """Return whether the CuTeDSL forward kernel can run on ``device``.
130	
131	  CuTeDSL forward supports SM90 through the existing Hopper specialised path
132	  for ``head_dim <= 512`` and falls back to the SM80 Ampere Split-D path for
133	  every other supported architecture (SM80/SM89, SM100/SM103/SM120, ...) and
134	  for any ``head_dim > 512``. Other backend constraints (head_dim ceiling,
135	  dtype, no mask/dropout) are enforced per-call by
136	  :func:`_require_cute_supported`; this only checks the device-level
137	  prerequisite so callers can pre-select a backend before allocating tensors.
138	  """
139	  if not torch.cuda.is_available():
140	    return False
141	  if device is None:
142	    device = torch.device("cuda", torch.cuda.current_device())
143	  if device.type != "cuda":
144	    return False
145	  major, _ = torch.cuda.get_device_capability(device)
146	  return major >= 8
147	
148	
149	def cute_max_supported_head_dim(device: Optional[torch.device] = None) -> int:
150	  """Return the current CuTeDSL dense head-dim ceiling for ``device``.
151	
152	  The SM80 Ampere Split-D implementation acts as a cross-architecture
153	  fallback and supports up to ``SM80_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM`` on every
154	  supported architecture. SM90 keeps the existing specialised path only
155	  in the ``head_dim <= SM90_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM`` range, but the SM80
156	  fallback covers the rest, so the effective ceiling is the SM80 value
157	  everywhere. ``device`` is kept for API compatibility but is unused.
158	  """
159	  del device
160	  return SM80_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM
161	
162	
163	def cute_backward_available(device: Optional[torch.device] = None) -> bool:
164	  """Whether the CuTeDSL backward kernel can run on ``device``.
165	
166	  Mirrors :func:`cute_forward_available`: SM90 keeps the existing Hopper
167	  specialised backward for ``head_dim <= 512``; every other supported
168	  architecture (SM80/SM89, SM100/SM103/SM120, ...) and every
169	  ``head_dim > 512`` uses the SM80 Ampere Split-D backward as a fallback.
170	  """
171	  if not torch.cuda.is_available():
172	    return False
173	  if device is None:
174	    device = torch.device("cuda", torch.cuda.current_device())
175	  if device.type != "cuda":
176	    return False
177	  major, _ = torch.cuda.get_device_capability(device)
178	  return major >= 8
179	
180	
181	def _cute_device_major(device: torch.device) -> int:
182	  if device.type == "cuda" and torch.cuda.is_available():
183	    major, _ = torch.cuda.get_device_capability(device)
184	    return major
185	  return 9
186	
187	
188	def _use_sm90_specialized(major: int, head_dim: int, head_dim_v: int) -> bool:
189	  """Return ``True`` when the SM90 specialised cutedsl path should be used.
190	
191	  Single routing predicate shared by forward/backward dispatch and by the
192	  fake-mode varlen validator selection. The SM90 specialised kernels only
193	  cover Hopper with symmetric ``head_dim <= SM90_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM``;
194	  every other case (other archs, or ``head_dim > 512``, or asymmetric q/v
195	  head_dim) falls back to the SM80 Ampere Split-D path.
196	  """
197	  return (
198	    major == 9 and MIN_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM <= head_dim <= SM90_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM
199	    and MIN_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM <= head_dim_v <= SM90_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM
200	  )
201	
202	
203	def _forward_impl_for_device(
204	  device: torch.device, head_dim: int, head_dim_v: int
205	):
206	  major = _cute_device_major(device)
207	  if major < 8:
208	    raise NotImplementedError(
209	      f"cutedsl forward requires compute capability >= 8.0; got {major}.x"
210	    )
211	  if _use_sm90_specialized(major, head_dim, head_dim_v):
212	    return _ffpa_attn_forward_sm90
213	  return _ffpa_attn_forward_sm80
214

216	def _backward_impl_for_device(
217	  device: torch.device, head_dim: int, head_dim_v: int
218	):
219	  major = _cute_device_major(device)
220	  if major < 8:
221	    raise NotImplementedError(
222	      f"cutedsl backward requires compute capability >= 8.0; got {major}.x"
223	    )
224	  if _use_sm90_specialized(major, head_dim, head_dim_v):
225	    return _ffpa_attn_backward_sm90
226	  return _ffpa_attn_backward_sm80

229	def _require_cute_supported(
230	  q: torch.Tensor,
231	  k: torch.Tensor,
232	  v: torch.Tensor,
233	  *,
234	  requires_grad: bool,
235	) -> None:
236	  """Validate tensor-level constraints for the cutedsl backend.
237	
238	  Checks device, supported CUDA architecture, dense large head_dim, and q/k/v dtype. Kwarg-level
239	  functional compatibility (``dropout_p``,
240	  ``attn_mask``, FlashAttention-extension kwargs) is **not** the
241	  responsibility of this function; that lives in
242	  :func:`_check_supported_options`, applied by the entry shims
243	  (:func:`_ffpa_attn_forward_cute`, :func:`_ffpa_attn_varlen_cute`).
244	
245	  Raises ``NotImplementedError`` / ``RuntimeError`` / ``TypeError`` for
246	  any tensor-level violation so users who pass ``forward_backend='cutedsl'``
247	  see an actionable error rather than a deep kernel crash.
248	  """
249	  if q.device.type != "cuda":
250	    raise RuntimeError(
251	      f"cutedsl backend requires CUDA tensors, got device {q.device}"
252	    )
253	  if not torch.cuda.is_available():
254	    raise RuntimeError(
255	      "cutedsl backend requires a CUDA-capable build of PyTorch"
256	    )
257	  major, _ = torch.cuda.get_device_capability(q.device)
258	  if major < 8:
259	    raise NotImplementedError(
260	      f"cutedsl backend requires compute capability >= 8.0; got {major}.x"
261	    )
262	  head_dim_q = q.size(-1)
263	  head_dim_v = v.size(-1)
264	  use_sm90 = _use_sm90_specialized(major, head_dim_q, head_dim_v)
265	  min_head_dim = MIN_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM if use_sm90 else dense_min_supported_head_dim(
266	  )
267	  max_head_dim = (
268	    SM90_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM if use_sm90 else SM80_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM
269	  )
270	  if not (min_head_dim <= head_dim_q <= max_head_dim):
271	    raise NotImplementedError(
272	      f"cutedsl backend only supports dense head_dim in "
273	      f"[{min_head_dim}, {max_head_dim}]; got {head_dim_q}"
274	    )
275	  if not use_sm90 and head_dim_q % SM80_FWD_SPLIT_D_CHUNK != 0:
276	    raise NotImplementedError(
277	      f"cutedsl SM80 Split-D fallback requires head_dim divisible by "
278	      f"{SM80_FWD_SPLIT_D_CHUNK}; got {head_dim_q}"
279	    )
280	  if q.dtype not in (torch.float16, torch.bfloat16):
281	    raise TypeError(
282	      f"cutedsl backend requires torch.float16 or torch.bfloat16, got {q.dtype}"
283	    )
284	  del requires_grad
285	  if k.size(-1) != q.size(-1) or v.size(-1) != q.size(-1):
286	    raise NotImplementedError(
287	      f"cutedsl backend requires q/k/v to share head_dim={q.size(-1)}; "
288	      f"got k={k.size(-1)} v={v.size(-1)}"
289	    )
290	
291	
292	def _bhnd_to_bnhd(t: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
293	  """Reshape ``[B, H, N, D]`` (SDPA) to the CuTeDSL-native ``[B, N, H, D]`` (FA)."""
294	  return t.transpose(1, 2).contiguous()
295	
296	
297	def _bnhd_to_bhnd(t: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
298	  """Reverse of :func:`_bhnd_to_bnhd`: FA ``[B, N, H, D]`` → SDPA ``[B, H, N, D]``."""
299	  return t.transpose(1, 2).contiguous()
300	
301	
302	def _ffpa_attn_forward_cute(
303	  q: torch.Tensor,
304	  k: torch.Tensor,
305	  v: torch.Tensor,
306	  softmax_scale: float,
307	  causal: bool,
308	  *,
309	  return_lse: bool = True,
310	) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
311	  """CuTeDSL Split-D forward with SDPA-layout in/out.
312	
313	  Accepts ``[B, H, N, D]`` (SDPA) layout, transposes to the CuTeDSL-native
314	  ``[B, N, H, D]`` (FA) layout, calls the registered torch op
315	  ``torch.ops.ffpa_attn._fwd_cute``, and transposes the output back.
316	  ``lse`` is always in ``[B, H, N]`` shape and does not require a transpose.
317	
318	  Called from :meth:`_FFPAAttnFunc.forward` when the dispatch selects
319	  ``CuTeDSLBackend`` — the autograd boundary is owned by
320	  :class:`ffpa_attn.functional.FFPAAttnFunc`, not by this function.
321	
322	  :param q: Query tensor ``[B, H_q, N_q, D]``.
323	  :param k: Key tensor ``[B, H_kv, N_kv, D]``.
324	  :param v: Value tensor ``[B, H_kv, N_kv, D]``.
325	  :param softmax_scale: Pre-softmax scaling factor (already resolved, never None).
326	  :param causal: Whether causal masking is applied.
327	  :param return_lse: Always ``True`` when called from the training path so lse
328	      is saved for backward.
329	  :returns: ``(out, lse)`` where ``out`` is ``[B, H_q, N_q, D]`` and
330	      ``lse`` is ``[B, H_q, N_q]`` float32.
331	  """
332	  requires_grad = any(t.requires_grad for t in (q, k, v))
333	  _require_cute_supported(q, k, v, requires_grad=requires_grad)
334	
335	  q_nhd, k_nhd, v_nhd = (_bhnd_to_bnhd(t) for t in (q, k, v))
336	  out_nhd, lse = torch.ops.ffpa_attn._fwd_cute(
337	    q_nhd,
338	    k_nhd,
339	    v_nhd,
340	    softmax_scale,
341	    int(causal),
342	    int(return_lse),
343	  )
344	  out_bhnd = _bnhd_to_bhnd(out_nhd)
345	  return out_bhnd, lse
346	
347	
348	def _ffpa_attn_backward_cute(
349	  grad_out: torch.Tensor,
350	  q: torch.Tensor,
351	  k: torch.Tensor,
352	  v: torch.Tensor,
353	  out: torch.Tensor,
354	  lse: torch.Tensor,
355	  softmax_scale: float,
356	  causal: bool,
357	  *,
358	  grad_kv_storage_dtype: torch.dtype | None = None,
359	) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
360	  """CuTeDSL backward with SDPA-layout in/out.
361	
362	  Accepts all tensors in ``[B, H, N, D]`` (SDPA) layout, transposes to
363	  ``[B, N, H, D]`` (FA) for the registered torch op
364	  ``torch.ops.ffpa_attn._bwd_cute``, and transposes the gradient outputs
365	  back to SDPA layout.
366	
367	  Called from :meth:`_FFPAAttnFunc.backward` when the dispatch selects
368	  ``CuTeDSLBackend``.
369	
370	  :param grad_out: Gradient w.r.t. output ``[B, H_q, N_q, D]``.
371	  :param q: Query tensor ``[B, H_q, N_q, D]`` (saved from forward).
372	  :param k: Key tensor ``[B, H_kv, N_kv, D]`` (saved from forward).
373	  :param v: Value tensor ``[B, H_kv, N_kv, D]`` (saved from forward).
374	  :param out: Output tensor ``[B, H_q, N_q, D]`` (saved from forward).
375	  :param lse: Log-sum-exp ``[B, H_q, N_q]`` float32 (saved from forward).
376	  :param softmax_scale: Pre-softmax scaling factor.
377	  :param causal: Whether causal masking was applied.
378	  :param grad_kv_storage_dtype: Optional ``torch.float32`` / ``torch.float16``
379	      storage dtype for the internal dK/dV HBM buffer; final returned
380	      gradients are always cast back to ``k.dtype`` / ``v.dtype``. ``None``
381	      keeps today's behaviour (buffer dtype = activation dtype).
382	  :returns: ``(dq, dk, dv)`` all in ``[B, H, N, D]`` SDPA layout.
383	  """
384	  q_nhd, k_nhd, v_nhd, out_nhd, dout_nhd = (
385	    _bhnd_to_bnhd(t) for t in (q, k, v, out, grad_out)
386	  )
387	  dq_nhd, dk_nhd, dv_nhd = torch.ops.ffpa_attn._bwd_cute(
388	    dout_nhd,
389	    q_nhd,
390	    k_nhd,
391	    v_nhd,
392	    out_nhd,
393	    lse,
394	    softmax_scale,
395	    int(causal),
396	    _grad_kv_storage_dtype_to_code(grad_kv_storage_dtype),
397	  )
398	  dq, dk, dv = (_bnhd_to_bhnd(t) for t in (dq_nhd, dk_nhd, dv_nhd))
399	  return dq, dk, dv
400	
401	
402	def _ffpa_attn_varlen_cute(
403	  q: torch.Tensor,
404	  k: torch.Tensor,
405	  v: torch.Tensor,
406	  cu_seqlens_q: torch.Tensor,
407	  cu_seqlens_k: torch.Tensor | None,
408	  max_seqlen_q: int,
409	  max_seqlen_k: int,
410	  *,
411	  dropout_p: float,
412	  softmax_scale: float | None,
413	  causal: bool,
414	  enable_gqa: bool,
415	  return_lse: bool,
416	  kwargs: dict,
417	) -> torch.Tensor | tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
418	  """Packed THD cutedsl entry. The varlen path bypasses
419	  :class:`ffpa_attn.functional.FFPAAttnFunc` and is autograd-registered via
420	  the ``ffpa_attn::_varlen_fwd_cute`` torch op directly.
421	
422	  CuTeDSL varlen forward called from
423	  :func:`ffpa_attn.ffpa_attn_interface.ffpa_attn_varlen_func`. The CuTeDSL
424	  kernel consumes packed ``[T, H, D]`` layout natively — no transpose, no
425	  per-sequence loop.
426	  """
427	  _check_supported_options(
428	    source="ffpa_attn_varlen_func",
429	    dropout_p=dropout_p,
430	    window_size=kwargs.get("window_size"),
431	    sink=kwargs.get("sink"),
432	    attention_mask=kwargs.get("attention_mask", kwargs.get("attn_mask")),
433	    block_mask=kwargs.get("block_mask"),
434	    softcap=kwargs.get("softcap"),
435	    score_mod=kwargs.get("score_mod"),
436	    aux_tensors=kwargs.get("aux_tensors"),
437	    seqused_k=kwargs.get("seqused_k"),
438	    block_table=kwargs.get("block_table"),
439	    num_splits=kwargs.get("num_splits"),
440	    alibi_slopes=kwargs.get("alibi_slopes"),
441	  )
442	
443	  if q.dim() != 3 or k.dim() != 3 or v.dim() != 3:
444	    raise ValueError(
445	      f"ffpa_attn_varlen_func: q/k/v must be 3-D packed [T, H, D], "
446	      f"got ranks q={q.dim()} k={k.dim()} v={v.dim()}"
447	    )
448	  if k.shape != v.shape:
449	    raise ValueError(
450	      f"ffpa_attn_varlen_func: k/v must share shape, "
451	      f"got k={tuple(k.shape)} v={tuple(v.shape)}"
452	    )
453	  if q.dtype not in (torch.float16, torch.bfloat16):
454	    raise TypeError(
455	      f"ffpa_attn_varlen_func: q/k/v must be fp16/bf16, got {q.dtype}"
456	    )
457	  if k.dtype != q.dtype or v.dtype != q.dtype:
458	    raise TypeError(
459	      f"ffpa_attn_varlen_func: q/k/v must share dtype, got "
460	      f"q={q.dtype} k={k.dtype} v={v.dtype}"

582	def _grad_kv_storage_dtype_to_code(dtype: torch.dtype | None, ) -> int:
583	  """Encode the dK/dV storage dtype selector for the torch op boundary.
584	
585	  Matches Triton's encoding: ``0=None`` (use activation dtype),
586	  ``1=fp32``, ``2=fp16``.
587	  """
588	  if dtype is None:
589	    return 0
590	  if dtype == torch.float32:
591	    return 1
592	  if dtype == torch.float16:
593	    return 2
594	  raise ValueError(
595	    f"Unsupported grad_kv_storage_dtype {dtype!r}; expected None, "
596	    "torch.float32, or torch.float16."
597	  )
598

600	def _grad_kv_storage_dtype_from_code(code: int) -> torch.dtype | None:
601	  """Decode the dK/dV storage dtype selector at the torch op boundary."""
602	  if code == 0:
603	    return None
604	  if code == 1:
605	    return torch.float32
606	  if code == 2:
607	    return torch.float16
608	  raise ValueError(
609	    f"Unsupported grad_kv_storage_dtype code {code}; expected 0, 1, or 2."
610	  )

622	  causal: int,

625	  storage_dtype = _grad_kv_storage_dtype_from_code(grad_kv_storage_dtype_code)
626	  backward_impl = _backward_impl_for_device(q.device, q.size(-1), v.size(-1))
627	  if storage_dtype is not None and backward_impl is not _ffpa_attn_backward_sm80:

631	      f"dK/dV storage-dtype override (head_dim={q.size(-1)}, "

633	      f"{torch.cuda.get_device_capability(q.device)[0]})."
634	    )

636	  if storage_dtype is None:
637	    dk = torch.empty_like(k)
638	    dv = torch.empty_like(v)
639	  else:
640	    dk = torch.empty_like(k, dtype=storage_dtype)
641	    dv = torch.empty_like(v, dtype=storage_dtype)
642	  backward_impl(
643	    q,
644	    k,
645	    v,

649	    softmax_scale=softmax_scale,

682	
683	def _trim_trailing_empty_varlen_segments(
684	  cu_seqlens_q: torch.Tensor,
685	  cu_seqlens_k: torch.Tensor,
686	) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
687	  """Drop trailing segments where both q and k have zero length."""
688	  if cu_seqlens_q.numel() != cu_seqlens_k.numel():
689	    return cu_seqlens_q, cu_seqlens_k
690	  if cu_seqlens_q.numel() <= 1 or is_fake_mode():
691	    return cu_seqlens_q, cu_seqlens_k
692	
693	  q_lengths = cu_seqlens_q[1:] - cu_seqlens_q[:-1]
694	  k_lengths = cu_seqlens_k[1:] - cu_seqlens_k[:-1]
695	  active = (q_lengths != 0) | (k_lengths != 0)
696	  if bool(active.all().item()):
697	    return cu_seqlens_q, cu_seqlens_k
698	  if not bool(active.any().item()):
699	    return cu_seqlens_q[:1], cu_seqlens_k[:1]
700	
701	  last_active_segment = int(active.nonzero()[-1].item())
702	  keep_numel = last_active_segment + 2
703	  if keep_numel == cu_seqlens_q.numel():
704	    return cu_seqlens_q, cu_seqlens_k
705	  return cu_seqlens_q[:keep_numel], cu_seqlens_k[:keep_numel]

45	def _make_fake_bwd_preprocess_tensors(dtype, varlen_q):
46	  sym = cute.sym_int

49	  seqlen_q_rounded = sym()

51	  q_shape = (batch, seqlen_q) if not varlen_q else (total_q, )
52	  mO = fake_tensor(dtype, (*q_shape, num_head, head_dim_v), divisibility=div)
53	  mdO = fake_tensor(dtype, (*q_shape, num_head, head_dim_v), divisibility=div)
54	  if not varlen_q:

156	def _torch_to_cute_storage_dtype(dtype: torch.dtype, ) -> Type[cutlass.Numeric]:

575	    )

612	      ) if dlse is not None else None

627	        v_seg,
628	        out_seg,

630	        lse_seg,
631	        softmax_scale,
632	        causal,
633	        dq_seg,
634	        dk_seg,
635	        dv_seg,
636	        dlse_seg,

48	def _make_fake_bwd_preprocess_tensors(dtype, varlen_q):
49	  sym = cute.sym_int
50	  div = 128 // dtype.width  # 8 for fp16/bf16
51	  b, seqlen_q, h_q, d_v = sym(), sym(), sym(), sym()
52	  seqlen_q_rounded = sym()
53	  total_q, total_q_rounded = sym(), sym()
54	  b_seqlenq = (b, seqlen_q) if not varlen_q else (total_q, )
55	  mO = fake_tensor(dtype, (*b_seqlenq, h_q, d_v), divisibility=div)
56	  mdO = fake_tensor(dtype, (*b_seqlenq, h_q, d_v), divisibility=div)
57	  if not varlen_q:
58	    mLSE = fake_tensor(Float32, (b, h_q, seqlen_q), divisibility=1)
59	    mLSElog2 = fake_tensor(Float32, (b, h_q, seqlen_q_rounded), divisibility=4)
60	    mPdPsum = fake_tensor(Float32, (b, h_q, seqlen_q_rounded), divisibility=4)
61	  else:
62	    mLSE = fake_tensor(Float32, (h_q, total_q), divisibility=1)
63	    mLSElog2 = fake_tensor(Float32, (h_q, total_q_rounded), divisibility=4)
64	    mPdPsum = fake_tensor(Float32, (h_q, total_q_rounded), divisibility=4)
65	  return mO, mdO, mLSE, mLSElog2, mPdPsum

367	      k_t,
368	      v_t,

10	# Methods (SM90 call sites in this repo, plus reserved helpers for future masked-iteration splits):
11	#   - get_n_block_min_max:              _fwd_d512_sm90.py (lines 798, 1005, 1425),
12	#                                       _dq_d512_sm90.py  (lines 759, 1015, 1317)
13	#   - get_m_block_min_max:              _dkdv_d512_sm90.py (lines 702, 963, 1211)

17	from typing import Tuple, Optional
18	from dataclasses import dataclass
19	
20	import cutlass
21	import cutlass.cute as cute
22	from cutlass import Int32, const_expr
23	
24	from .seqlen_info import SeqlenInfoQK
25	
26	
27	@dataclass(frozen=True)
28	class BlockInfo:
29	  tile_m: cutlass.Constexpr[int]
30	  tile_n: cutlass.Constexpr[int]
31	  is_causal: cutlass.Constexpr[bool]
32	  is_local: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False
33	  window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None
34	  window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None
35	  qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa: cutlass.Constexpr[int] = 1
36	
37	  @cute.jit
38	  def get_n_block_min_max(
39	    self,
40	    seqlen_info: SeqlenInfoQK,
41	    m_block: Int32,
42	  ) -> Tuple[Int32, Int32]:
43	    n_block_max = cute.ceil_div(seqlen_info.seqlen_k, self.tile_n)
44	    if const_expr(
45	      self.is_causal or (self.is_local and self.window_size_right is not None)
46	    ):
47	      m_idx_max = (m_block + 1) * self.tile_m
48	      if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa > 1):
49	        m_idx_max = cute.ceil_div(m_idx_max, self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa)
50	      n_idx = m_idx_max + seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
51	      n_idx_right = n_idx if const_expr(
52	        self.is_causal
53	      ) else n_idx + self.window_size_right
54	      n_block_max = min(n_block_max, cute.ceil_div(n_idx_right, self.tile_n))
55	    n_block_min = 0
56	    if const_expr(self.is_local and self.window_size_left is not None):
57	      m_idx_min = m_block * self.tile_m
58	      if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa > 1):
59	        m_idx_min = m_idx_min // self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
60	      n_idx = m_idx_min + seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
61	      n_idx_left = n_idx - self.window_size_left
62	      n_block_min = cutlass.max(n_idx_left // self.tile_n, 0)
63	    return n_block_min, n_block_max
64	
65	  @cute.jit
66	  def get_m_block_min_max(self, seqlen_info: SeqlenInfoQK,
67	                          n_block: Int32) -> Tuple[Int32, Int32]:
68	    m_block_max = cute.ceil_div(seqlen_info.seqlen_q, self.tile_m)
69	    m_block_min = 0
70	    if const_expr(
71	      self.is_causal or (self.is_local and self.window_size_right is not None)
72	    ):
73	      n_idx_min = n_block * self.tile_n
74	      m_idx = n_idx_min + seqlen_info.seqlen_q - seqlen_info.seqlen_k
75	      m_idx_right = m_idx if const_expr(
76	        self.is_causal
77	      ) else m_idx - self.window_size_right
78	      m_block_min = max(m_block_min, m_idx_right // self.tile_m)
79	    if const_expr(self.is_local and self.window_size_left is not None):
80	      n_idx_max = (n_block + 1) * self.tile_n
81	      m_idx = n_idx_max + seqlen_info.seqlen_q - seqlen_info.seqlen_k
82	      m_idx_left = m_idx + self.window_size_left
83	      m_block_max = min(m_block_max, cute.ceil_div(m_idx_left, self.tile_m))
84	    return m_block_min, m_block_max
85	
86	  # Reserved: for casual and sliding window case : lower bound of the n-block range that still needs causal / right-local masking.
87	  @cute.jit
88	  def get_n_block_min_causal_local_mask(
89	    self,
90	    seqlen_info: SeqlenInfoQK,
91	    m_block: Int32,
92	    n_block_min: Int32,
93	  ) -> Int32:
94	    """If we have separate iterations with causal or local masking at the start, where do we stop"""
95	    m_idx_min = m_block * self.tile_m
96	    if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa > 1):
97	      m_idx_min = m_idx_min // self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
98	    n_idx = m_idx_min + seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
99	    n_idx_right = (
100	      n_idx if const_expr(not self.is_local or self.window_size_right is None)
101	      else n_idx + self.window_size_right
102	    )
103	    return cutlass.max(n_block_min, n_idx_right // self.tile_n)
104	
105	  # Reserved: for sliding window case : lower bound of the no-mask fast path, i.e. the n-block at which left-local masking begins.
106	  @cute.jit
107	  def get_n_block_min_before_local_mask(
108	    self,
109	    seqlen_info: SeqlenInfoQK,
110	    m_block: Int32,
111	    n_block_min: Int32,
112	  ) -> Int32:
113	    """If we have separate iterations with local masking at the end, where do we stop the non-masked iterations"""
114	    if const_expr(not self.is_local or self.window_size_left is None):
115	      return n_block_min
116	    else:
117	      m_idx_max = (m_block + 1) * self.tile_m
118	      if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa > 1):
119	        m_idx_max = cute.ceil_div(m_idx_max, self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa)
120	      n_idx = m_idx_max + seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
121	      n_idx_left = n_idx - self.window_size_left
122	      return cutlass.max(n_block_min, cute.ceil_div(n_idx_left, self.tile_n))

211	class SingleTileLPTScheduler:

213	  @dataclass

232	      loc=None,
233	      ip=None,
234	    ) -> "SingleTileLPTScheduler.Params":
235	      size_one_kv_head = args.seqlen_k * (

239	      size_l2 = 50 * 1024 * 1024  # 40 MB for K & V
240	      # Swizzle is the size of each "section". Round swizzle to a power of 2
241	      # swizzle is how many heads can fit in L2

245	      )

247	      # swizzle. Instead we want to divide by the remainder.
248	      num_hb_quotient = (args.num_head * args.num_batch) // swizzle
249	      num_hb_remainder = (args.num_head * args.num_batch) % swizzle
250	      return SingleTileLPTScheduler.Params(

252	        num_block=args.num_block,
253	        num_head=args.num_head,

258	        l2_major_divmod=FastDivmodDivisor(swizzle * args.num_block),
259	        l2_minor_residual_divmod=FastDivmodDivisor(max(num_hb_remainder, 1)),
260	        num_hb_quotient=Int32(num_hb_quotient),

600	    total_blocks_max = (
601	      params.total_q + params.num_batch * (params.tile_shape_mn[0] - 1)

605	  @cute.jit
606	  def _get_num_m_blocks(self, lane: Int32, bidb_start: Int32) -> Int32:
607	    params = self.params
608	    batch_idx = lane + bidb_start
609	    if cutlass.const_expr(params.mSeqUsedQ is not None):
610	      seqlen = Int32(0)
611	      if batch_idx < params.num_batch:
612	        seqlen = params.mSeqUsedQ[batch_idx]
613	    else:
614	      assert params.mCuSeqlensQ is not None
615	      cur_cu_seqlen = Int32(0)
616	      if batch_idx <= params.num_batch:
617	        cur_cu_seqlen = params.mCuSeqlensQ[batch_idx]
618	      next_cu_seqlen = cute.arch.shuffle_sync_down(cur_cu_seqlen, offset=1)
619	      seqlen = next_cu_seqlen - cur_cu_seqlen
620	    if cutlass.const_expr(params.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa > 1):
621	      seqlen *= params.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
622	    # cluster_shape_m=1 for SM90, so no outer ceil_div by cluster
623	    return (
624	      cute.ceil_div(seqlen, params.tile_shape_mn[0])
625	      if batch_idx < params.num_batch and lane < cute.arch.WARP_SIZE - 1 else
626	      Int32(0)
627	    )

629	  @cute.jit
630	  def _varlen_coord_map(self) -> WorkTileInfo:
631	    """Map self._tile_idx to (block, head, batch) via warp-level prefix sums."""
632	    params = self.params
633	    lane_idx = cute.arch.lane_idx()
634	    num_m_blocks = self._get_num_m_blocks(lane_idx, bidb_start=0)
635	    num_m_blocks_cumulative = warp_prefix_sum(num_m_blocks, lane_idx)
636	    # Total number of blocks for the next 31 batches
637	    m_blocks_in_group = cute.arch.shuffle_sync(
638	      num_m_blocks_cumulative, cute.arch.WARP_SIZE - 1
639	    )
640	    # Same for all lanes
641	    group_end_tile = m_blocks_in_group * params.num_head
642	    block, head_idx, batch_idx = Int32(0), Int32(0), Int32(0)
643	    # cluster_shape_m=1 for SM90, so next_tile_idx == self._tile_idx
644	    next_tile_idx = self._tile_idx
645	    while group_end_tile <= next_tile_idx:
646	      batch_idx += cute.arch.WARP_SIZE - 1
647	      if batch_idx >= params.num_batch:
648	        batch_idx = Int32(params.num_batch)
649	        group_end_tile = next_tile_idx + 1
650	      else:
651	        num_m_blocks = self._get_num_m_blocks(lane_idx, bidb_start=batch_idx)
652	        num_m_blocks_cumulative = warp_prefix_sum(num_m_blocks, lane_idx)
653	        m_blocks_in_group = cute.arch.shuffle_sync(
654	          num_m_blocks_cumulative, cute.arch.WARP_SIZE - 1
655	        )
656	        group_end_tile += m_blocks_in_group * params.num_head
657	    is_valid = False
658	    if batch_idx >= params.num_batch:
659	      block, head_idx, batch_idx = Int32(0), Int32(0), Int32(params.num_batch)
660	    else:
661	      group_start_tile = group_end_tile - m_blocks_in_group * params.num_head
662	      # The next problem to process is the first one that does not have ending tile position
663	      # that is greater than or equal to tile index.
664	      batch_idx_in_group = cute.arch.popc(
665	        cute.arch.vote_ballot_sync(
666	          group_start_tile +
667	          num_m_blocks_cumulative * params.num_head <= next_tile_idx
668	        )
669	      )
670	      batch_idx += batch_idx_in_group
671	      num_m_blocks_prev_lane = (
672	        0 if batch_idx_in_group == 0 else
673	        cute.arch.shuffle_sync(num_m_blocks_cumulative, batch_idx_in_group - 1)
674	      )
675	      num_m_blocks = cute.arch.shuffle_sync(num_m_blocks, batch_idx_in_group)
676	      mh_block = next_tile_idx - group_start_tile - num_m_blocks_prev_lane * params.num_head
677	      if cutlass.const_expr(params.lpt or params.head_swizzle):
678	        # L2-aware LPT scheduling within variable-length batches.
679	        # cluster_shape_m=1 for SM90, simplifies num_n_blocks calculation.
680	        num_n_blocks = (
681	          num_m_blocks * params.tile_shape_mn[0] //
682	          params.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa // params.tile_shape_mn[1]
683	        )
684	        # Seems faster to have this be a power of 2
685	        nheads_in_l2 = (
686	          16 if num_n_blocks * 16 <= params.max_kvblock_in_l2 else (
687	            8 if num_n_blocks * 8 <= params.max_kvblock_in_l2 else (
688	              4 if num_n_blocks * 4 <= params.max_kvblock_in_l2 else
689	              (2 if num_n_blocks * 2 <= params.max_kvblock_in_l2 else 1)
690	            )
691	          )
692	        )
693	        nheads_in_l2 = min(nheads_in_l2, params.num_head)
694	        mh_in_l2 = nheads_in_l2 * num_m_blocks
695	        section_idx = mh_block // mh_in_l2
696	        l2_mod = mh_block - section_idx * mh_in_l2
697	        # Deal with tail section
698	        nheads_in_this_section = (
699	          nheads_in_l2 if nheads_in_l2 *
700	          (section_idx + 1) <= params.num_head else params.num_head -
701	          section_idx * nheads_in_l2
702	        )
703	        block = l2_mod // nheads_in_this_section
704	        head_idx_residual = l2_mod - block * nheads_in_this_section
705	        head_idx = section_idx * nheads_in_l2 + head_idx_residual
706	        if cutlass.const_expr(params.lpt):
707	          block = num_m_blocks - 1 - block
708	      else:
709	        head_idx = mh_block // num_m_blocks
710	        block = mh_block - head_idx * num_m_blocks
711	      is_valid = self._is_first_block and batch_idx < params.num_batch
712	      # cluster_shape_m=1 for SM90, no bidx_in_cluster adjustment needed
713	    return WorkTileInfo(
714	      (Int32(block), Int32(head_idx), Int32(batch_idx), Int32(0)), is_valid
715	    )

18	def _override_create(parent_cls, child_cls):
19	  """Create a static factory that constructs parent_cls then re-classes to child_cls."""
20	
21	  @staticmethod
22	  def create(*args, **kwargs):
23	    obj = parent_cls.create(*args, **kwargs)
24	    # Can't assign to __class__ directly since the dataclass is frozen
25	    object.__setattr__(obj, "__class__", child_cls)
26	    return obj
27	
28	  return create

31	def _make_state(index: Int32, phase: Int32) -> PipelineState:
32	  """Construct a PipelineState from index and phase (count/stages unused by callers)."""
33	  return PipelineState(stages=0, count=Int32(0), index=index, phase=phase)

36	# Reserved: alternative pipeline state encoding (single Int32 for index+phase).
37	# Current SM90 kernels use cutlass.pipeline.PipelineState directly; this remains
38	# for future ports that need the compact encoding.
39	class PipelineStateSimple:
40	  """
41	    Pipeline state contains an index and phase bit corresponding to the current position in the circular buffer.
42	    Use a single Int32 to store both the index and phase bit, then we use divmod to get the
43	    index and phase. If stages is a power of 2, divmod turns into bit twiddling.
44	    """
45	
46	  def __init__(self, stages: int, phase_index: Int32):
47	    self._stages = stages
48	    self._phase_index = phase_index
49	
50	  def clone(self) -> "PipelineStateSimple":
51	    return PipelineStateSimple(self.stages, self._phase_index)
52	
53	  @property
54	  def stages(self) -> int:
55	    return self._stages
56	
57	  @property
58	  def index(self) -> Int32:
59	    if const_expr(self._stages == 1):
60	      return Int32(0)
61	    else:
62	      return self._phase_index % self._stages
63	
64	  @property
65	  def phase(self) -> Int32:
66	    # PTX docs say that the phase parity needs to be 0 or 1, so by right we need to
67	    # take modulo 2. But in practice just passing the phase in without modulo works fine.
68	    if const_expr(self._stages == 1):
69	      return self._phase_index
70	    else:
71	      return self._phase_index // self._stages
72	
73	  def advance(self):
74	    if const_expr(self._stages == 1):
75	      self._phase_index ^= 1
76	    else:
77	      self._phase_index += 1
78	
79	  def __extract_mlir_values__(self):
80	    phase_index = self._phase_index
81	    return [phase_index.ir_value()]
82	
83	  def __new_from_mlir_values__(self, values):
84	    return PipelineStateSimple(self.stages, Int32(values[0]))
85	
86	
87	# Reserved: factory for PipelineStateSimple; see PipelineStateSimple for the
88	# reservation rationale.
89	def make_pipeline_state(type: PipelineUserType, stages: int):
90	  """
91	    Creates a pipeline state. Producers are assumed to start with an empty buffer and have a flipped phase bit of 1.
92	    """
93	  if type is PipelineUserType.Producer:
94	    return PipelineStateSimple(stages, Int32(stages))
95	  elif type is PipelineUserType.Consumer:
96	    return PipelineStateSimple(stages, Int32(0))
97	  else:
98	    assert False, "Error: invalid PipelineUserType specified for make_pipeline_state."

104	def _call_with_elect_one(
105	  parent_method, self, state, elect_one, syncwarp, loc, ip
106	):
107	  """Optionally wrap a parent pipeline method call in sync_warp + elect_one."""
108	  if const_expr(elect_one):
109	    if const_expr(syncwarp):
110	      cute.arch.sync_warp()
111	    with cute.arch.elect_one():
112	      parent_method(self, state, loc=loc, ip=ip)
113	  else:
114	    parent_method(self, state, loc=loc, ip=ip)

208	class PipelineAsync(_PipelineIndexPhaseMixin, PipelineAsyncOg):
209	  """
210	    PipelineAsync with optional elect_one for producer_commit and consumer_release.
211	
212	    When elect_one_*=True (set at create time), only one elected thread per warp
213	    signals the barrier arrive. This is useful when the mask count is set to 1 per warp.
214	
215	    Args (to create):
216	        elect_one_commit: If True, only elected thread signals producer_commit.
217	        syncwarp_before_commit: If True (default), issue syncwarp before elect_one.
218	        elect_one_release: If True, only elected thread signals consumer_release.
219	        syncwarp_before_release: If True (default), issue syncwarp before elect_one.
220	            Set syncwarp to False when threads are already converged (e.g. after wgmma wait_group).
221	    """

223	  _elect_one_commit: bool = False
224	  _syncwarp_before_commit: bool = True
225	  _elect_one_release: bool = False
226	  _syncwarp_before_release: bool = True

229	  def create(
230	    *args,
231	    elect_one_commit: bool = False,
232	    syncwarp_before_commit: bool = True,
233	    elect_one_release: bool = False,
234	    syncwarp_before_release: bool = True,
235	    **kwargs,

238	    object.__setattr__(obj, "__class__", PipelineAsync)
239	    object.__setattr__(obj, "_elect_one_commit", elect_one_commit)
240	    object.__setattr__(obj, "_syncwarp_before_commit", syncwarp_before_commit)

315	@dataclass(frozen=True)
316	class PipelineTmaAsync(_PipelineIndexPhaseMixin, PipelineTmaAsyncOg):
317	  """Override producer_acquire to take in extra_tx_count parameter."""
318	
319	  @dsl_user_op
320	  def producer_acquire(
321	    self,
322	    state: PipelineState,
323	    try_acquire_token: Optional[Boolean] = None,
324	    extra_tx_count: int = 0,
325	    *,
326	    loc=None,
327	    ip=None,
328	  ):
329	    """
330	        TMA producer commit conditionally waits on buffer empty and sets the transaction barrier for leader threadblocks.
331	        """
332	    if_generate(
333	      try_acquire_token is None or try_acquire_token == 0,
334	      lambda: self.sync_object_empty.
335	      wait(state.index, state.phase, loc=loc, ip=ip),
336	      loc=loc,
337	      ip=ip,
338	    )
339	    if const_expr(extra_tx_count == 0):
340	      self.sync_object_full.arrive(
341	        state.index, self.producer_mask, loc=loc, ip=ip
342	      )
343	    else:
344	      tx_count = self.sync_object_full.tx_count + extra_tx_count
345	      self.sync_object_full.arrive_and_expect_tx(
346	        state.index, tx_count, loc=loc, ip=ip
347	      )
348

11	#   - r2p_bitmask_below / r2p_bitmask_above (R2P bitmask primitives)
12	#   - mask_r2p_lambda (R2P masking kernel)
13	#   - sm90_col_to_r2p_idx (SM90 MMA column-to-R2P coordinate transform)
14	#
15	# AttentionMask.apply_mask call sites in this repo:
16	#   - _fwd_d512_sm90.py:982   (seqlen-only / causal / local / mask_mod / PackGQA)
17	#   - _dq_d512_sm90.py:1027, 1330   (seqlen-only / causal)
18	#   - _dkdv_d512_sm90.py:982, 1233  (seqlen-only / causal)
19	#
20	# swap_AB=True branch of apply_mask is reserved for a future SM90 bwd SdP swap_AB path
21	# (no current call sites). See `swap_AB` field below and the swap_AB arm inside apply_mask.
22	#
23	# Dependencies:
24	#   - quack.layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn (MMA accumulator reshape)
25	#   - flash_attn.cute.utils: shr_u32, shl_u32, scalar_to_ssa, ssa_to_scalar, shuffle_sync

28	from typing import Optional, Callable, TypeAlias

33	from cutlass import Float32, Int32, Uint32, const_expr

36	from . import shr_u32, shl_u32, shuffle_sync, scalar_to_ssa, ssa_to_scalar

39	MaskGenFn: TypeAlias = Callable[[int], Uint32]
40	MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE: int = 32
41	
42	
43	@cute.jit
44	def r2p_bitmask_below(limit: Int32, s: int) -> Uint32:
45	  """32-bit R2P bitmask keeping positions < limit (exclusive upper bound).
46	
47	    Positions 0..limit-1 in chunk `s` get bit=1 (keep), the rest bit=0 (mask).
48	    Uses inline PTX to avoid shift-by-type-width UB.
49	    """
50	  m = max((s + 1) * MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE - limit, 0)
51	  return shr_u32(Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), Uint32(m))
52	
53	
54	@cute.jit
55	def r2p_bitmask_above(limit: Int32, s: int) -> Uint32:
56	  """32-bit R2P bitmask keeping positions >= limit (inclusive lower bound).
57	
58	    Positions limit..31 in chunk `s` get bit=1 (keep), the rest bit=0 (mask).
59	    Uses inline PTX to avoid shift-by-type-width UB.
60	    """
61	  n = max(limit - s * MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE, 0)
62	  return shl_u32(Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), Uint32(n))
63	
64	
65	@cute.jit
66	def mask_r2p_lambda(
67	  X: cute.Tensor,
68	  mask_gen_fn: cutlass.Constexpr[MaskGenFn],
69	  rank1: bool = False,
70	) -> None:
71	  """Apply R2P masking with a custom bitmask generator.
72	
73	    mask_gen_fn(chunk_idx: constexpr int) -> Uint32:
74	        Returns a 32-bit bitmask for the chunk. Bit i set means column
75	        chunk_idx * chunk_size + i is KEPT; bit i clear means masked to -inf.
76	    """
77	  ncol = const_expr(
78	    cute.size(X.shape[cute.rank(X) - 1]) if not rank1 else cute.size(X.shape)
79	  )
80	  # 32-column chunks. The mask_gen_fn returns a Uint32 bitmask (1=keep).
81	  CHUNK_SIZE = MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE
82	  for s in cutlass.range_constexpr(cute.ceil_div(ncol, CHUNK_SIZE)):
83	    mask = mask_gen_fn(s)
84	    # This needs to be range_constexpr, o/w the compiler can't generate the R2P instruction
85	    for i in cutlass.range_constexpr(min(CHUNK_SIZE, ncol - s * CHUNK_SIZE)):
86	      in_bound = cutlass.Boolean(mask & (Uint32(1) << i))
87	      c = s * CHUNK_SIZE + i
88	      if const_expr(rank1):
89	        X[c] = X[c] if in_bound else -Float32.inf
90	      else:
91	        for r in cutlass.range_constexpr(cute.size(X.shape[0])):
92	          X[r, c] = X[r, c] if in_bound else -Float32.inf
93	
94	
95	@cute.jit
96	def sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(col_limit: Int32) -> Int32:
97	  """Transform SM90 MMA column coordinate to R2P element index.
98	
99	    SM90 MMA accumulator column indices are non-contiguous: 0, 1, 8, 9, 16, 17, ...
100	    Element indices are contiguous: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ...
101	    This converts a column-space threshold to element-space for r2p_bitmask_below/above.
102	    """
103	  return col_limit // 8 * 2 + min(col_limit % 8, 2)
104

106	@dataclass(frozen=True)
107	class AttentionMask:
108	  tile_m: cutlass.Constexpr[int]
109	  tile_n: cutlass.Constexpr[int]
110	  seqlen_info: SeqlenInfoQK
111	  window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None
112	  window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None
113	  qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa: cutlass.Constexpr[
114	    int] = 1  # only pass in if we're doing PackGQA
115	  # Reserved: enables the bwd SdP swap_AB path of apply_mask (currently no SM90 call site sets this True).
116	  swap_AB: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False
117	
118	  @property
119	  def seqlen_q(self) -> Int32:
120	    return self.seqlen_info.seqlen_q
121	
122	  @property
123	  def seqlen_k(self) -> Int32:
124	    return self.seqlen_info.seqlen_k
125	
126	  @cute.jit
127	  def apply_mask(
128	    self,
129	    acc_S: cute.Tensor,
130	    batch_idx: cutlass.Int32,
131	    head_idx: cutlass.Int32,
132	    m_block: cutlass.Int32,
133	    n_block: cutlass.Int32,
134	    thr_mma: cute.TiledMma,
135	    mask_seqlen: cutlass.Constexpr[bool],
136	    mask_causal: cutlass.Constexpr[bool],
137	    mask_local: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
138	    mask_mod: cutlass.Constexpr[Optional[Callable]] = None,
139	    aux_tensors: Optional[list] = None,
140	    fastdiv_mods=(None, None),
141	  ) -> None:
142	    assert not (
143	      mask_causal and mask_local
144	    ), "mask_causal and mask_local cannot be both True"
145	    acc_S_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(acc_S, transpose=self.swap_AB)
146	    acc_shape = (self.tile_m, self.tile_n)
147	    cS = cute.make_identity_tensor(
148	      acc_shape if not self.swap_AB else acc_shape[::-1]
149	    )
150	    tScS_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(
151	      thr_mma.partition_C(cS), transpose=self.swap_AB
152	    )
153	    # We use t0ScS as these indices are known at compile time. We then must subtract the
154	    # column limit by the thread column offset.
155	    t0ScS_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(
156	      thr_mma.get_slice(0).partition_C(cS), transpose=self.swap_AB
157	    )
158	    ROW = 0 if const_expr(not self.swap_AB) else 1
159	    COL = 1 if const_expr(not self.swap_AB) else 0
160	    thr_col_offset = tScS_mn[0][COL]
161	    # To handle edge cases of completely masked out rows where n_block_max = 0,
162	    # we treat negative n_blocks as 0th n_block
163	    # TODO: find more transparent solution
164	    if n_block < 0:
165	      n_block = 0
166	    seqlenk_col_limit = self.seqlen_k - n_block * self.tile_n - thr_col_offset
167	    if const_expr(not mask_causal and not mask_local and mask_mod is None):
168	      if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
169	        r2p = const_expr(not self.swap_AB)
170	        if const_expr(not r2p):
171	          # traverse column index.
172	          for c in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True):
173	            oob = t0ScS_mn[0, c][COL] >= seqlenk_col_limit
174	            for r in cutlass.range(
175	              cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True
176	            ):
177	              acc_S_mn[r, c] = -Float32.inf if oob else acc_S_mn[r, c]
178	        else:
179	          seqlenk_col_limit_r2p = sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(seqlenk_col_limit)
180	          mask_r2p_lambda(
181	            acc_S_mn, lambda s: r2p_bitmask_below(seqlenk_col_limit_r2p, s)
182	          )
183	
184	    elif const_expr(
185	      not mask_causal and not mask_local and mask_mod is not None
186	    ):  # FlexAttention mask mod
187	      nrow = const_expr(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]))
188	      ncol = const_expr(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]))
189	      has_fastdiv = const_expr(
190	        fastdiv_mods is not None and fastdiv_mods[0] is not None
191	        and fastdiv_mods[1] is not None
192	      )
193	      wrap_aux_indices = const_expr(
194	        has_fastdiv and mask_seqlen and const_expr(aux_tensors is not None)
195	      )
196	
197	      for r in cutlass.range_constexpr(nrow):
198	        # Respect swap_AB: ROW/COL determine which coordinate component corresponds to Q/KV.
199	        local_row = tScS_mn[r, 0][ROW]
200	        global_row_idx = local_row + m_block * self.tile_m
201	        row_for_mod = global_row_idx
202	        head_idx_for_mod = head_idx
203	        if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa != 1):
204	          head_offset = global_row_idx % self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
205	          head_idx_for_mod = head_idx * self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa + head_offset
206	          row_for_mod = global_row_idx // self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
207	        row_for_seqlen = row_for_mod
208	        if const_expr(wrap_aux_indices):
209	          _, row_for_mod = divmod(row_for_mod, fastdiv_mods[0])
210	
211	        for col in cutlass.range_constexpr(ncol):
212	          col_idx_local = t0ScS_mn[0, col][COL]
213	          # Convert to absolute column index
214	          global_col_idx = thr_col_offset + col_idx_local + n_block * self.tile_n
215	          col_for_mod = global_col_idx
216	          if const_expr(wrap_aux_indices):
217	            _, col_for_mod = divmod(global_col_idx, fastdiv_mods[1])
218	
219	          batch_idx_ssa = scalar_to_ssa(batch_idx, cutlass.Int32)
220	          head_idx_ssa = scalar_to_ssa(head_idx_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
221	          q_idx_ssa = scalar_to_ssa(row_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
222	          kv_idx_ssa = scalar_to_ssa(col_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
223	          mask_value = mask_mod(
224	            batch_idx_ssa,
225	            head_idx_ssa,
226	            q_idx_ssa,
227	            kv_idx_ssa,
228	            self.seqlen_info,
229	            aux_tensors,
230	          )
231	          cond = cutlass.Boolean(ssa_to_scalar(mask_value))
232	          if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
233	            out_of_bounds = (row_for_seqlen >= self.seqlen_q
234	                             ) or (global_col_idx >= self.seqlen_k)
235	            if out_of_bounds:
236	              acc_S_mn[r, col] = -cutlass.Float32.inf
237	            else:
238	              acc_S_mn[r, col] = acc_S_mn[r,
239	                                          col] if cond else -cutlass.Float32.inf
240	          else:
241	            acc_S_mn[r, col] = acc_S_mn[r,
242	                                        col] if cond else -cutlass.Float32.inf
243	
244	    else:  # Causal or local
245	      if const_expr(not self.swap_AB):
246	        # If PackGQA, we split the work of compute divmod among threads in the same row
247	        threads_per_row = thr_mma.tv_layout_C.shape[0][0]
248	        mma_m_idx = None
249	        if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa != 1):
250	          assert not self.swap_AB, "swap_AB with PackGQA not supported yet"
251	          assert cute.arch.WARP_SIZE % threads_per_row == 0, "threads_per_row must divide WARP_SIZE"
252	          assert cute.size(acc_S_mn.shape[0]) <= threads_per_row
253	          tidx = thr_mma.thr_idx
254	          mma_m_idx = (
255	            m_block * self.tile_m + tScS_mn[tidx % threads_per_row, 0][0]
256	          ) // self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
257	        causal_row_offset = 1 + self.seqlen_k - n_block * self.tile_n - self.seqlen_q - thr_col_offset
258	        if const_expr(mask_causal):
259	          r2p = const_expr(not self.swap_AB)  # R2P trick, see apply_mask_sm100
260	          for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True):
261	            # get the column index limit based on current row. Only consider the row index, so the column index sets to 0.
262	            if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa == 1):
263	              row_idx = tScS_mn[r, 0][0] + m_block * self.tile_m
264	            else:
265	              row_idx = shuffle_sync(
266	                mma_m_idx, r % threads_per_row, width=threads_per_row
267	              )
268	            col_limit_right = row_idx + causal_row_offset
269	            if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
270	              col_limit_right = cutlass.min(col_limit_right, seqlenk_col_limit)
271	            if const_expr(not r2p):
272	              # traverse column index.
273	              for c in cutlass.range(
274	                cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True
275	              ):
276	                acc_S_mn[r, c] = (
277	                  -Float32.inf
278	                  if t0ScS_mn[0, c][1] >= col_limit_right else acc_S_mn[r, c]
279	                )
280	            else:
281	              col_limit_r2p = sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(col_limit_right)
282	              mask_r2p_lambda(
283	                acc_S_mn[r, None],
284	                lambda s: r2p_bitmask_below(col_limit_r2p, s),
285	                rank1=True,
286	              )
287	        else:  # Local
288	          local_row_offset_right = (
289	            causal_row_offset + self.window_size_right
290	            if const_expr(self.window_size_right is not None) else None
291	          )
292	          local_row_offset_left = (
293	            causal_row_offset - 1 - self.window_size_left
294	            if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None) else None
295	          )
296	          r2p_local = const_expr(not self.swap_AB)
297	          for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True):
298	            if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa == 1):
299	              row_idx = tScS_mn[r, 0][0] + m_block * self.tile_m
300	            else:
301	              row_idx = shuffle_sync(
302	                mma_m_idx, r % threads_per_row, width=threads_per_row
303	              )
304	            if const_expr(self.window_size_right is not None):
305	              col_limit_right = row_idx + local_row_offset_right
306	            else:
307	              col_limit_right = self.tile_n
308	            if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
309	              col_limit_right = cutlass.min(col_limit_right, seqlenk_col_limit)
310	            col_limit_left = (
311	              row_idx + local_row_offset_left
312	              if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None) else 0
313	            )
314	            if const_expr(not r2p_local):
315	              # traverse column index.
316	              for c in cutlass.range(
317	                cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True
318	              ):
319	                col_idx = t0ScS_mn[0, c][1]
320	                if col_idx >= col_limit_right or col_idx < col_limit_left:
321	                  acc_S_mn[r, c] = -Float32.inf
322	            else:
323	              col_limit_right_r2p = sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(col_limit_right)
324	              col_limit_left_r2p = sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(col_limit_left)
325	
326	              def mask_gen_fn(s: int) -> Uint32:
327	                return r2p_bitmask_below(
328	                  col_limit_right_r2p, s
329	                ) & r2p_bitmask_above(col_limit_left_r2p, s)
330	
331	              mask_r2p_lambda(acc_S_mn[r, None], mask_gen_fn, rank1=True)
332	      # Reserved: future SM90 bwd SdP swap_AB path; not reached under current call sites.
333	      else:  # swap_AB (backward SdP path)
334	        assert self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa == 1
335	        thr_row_offset = tScS_mn[0][ROW]
336	        causal_row_offset = seqlenk_col_limit - self.seqlen_q + m_block * self.tile_m + thr_row_offset
337	        if const_expr(mask_causal):
338	          for c in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True):
339	            col0 = t0ScS_mn[0, c][COL]
340	            # If col0 is beyond the column limit, we want to mask out the entire
341	            # column, by setting row limit to be self.tile_m.
342	            row_limit_top = (
343	              self.tile_m if col0 >= seqlenk_col_limit and mask_seqlen else
344	              col0 - causal_row_offset
345	            )
346	            for r in cutlass.range(
347	              cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True
348	            ):
349	              acc_S_mn[r, c] = -Float32.inf if t0ScS_mn[
350	                r, 0][ROW] < row_limit_top else acc_S_mn[r, c]
351	        else:
352	          for c in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True):
353	            col0 = t0ScS_mn[0, c][COL]
354	            # If col0 is beyond the column limit, we want to mask out the entire
355	            # column, by setting row limit to be self.tile_m.
356	            row_limit_top = (
357	              self.tile_m if col0 >= seqlenk_col_limit and mask_seqlen else (
358	                col0 - causal_row_offset - self.window_size_right
359	                if const_expr(self.window_size_right is not None) else 0
360	              )
361	            )
362	            row_limit_bot = (
363	              col0 - causal_row_offset + self.window_size_left
364	              if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None) else self.tile_m
365	            )
366	            for r in cutlass.range(
367	              cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True
368	            ):
369	              row_idx = t0ScS_mn[r, 0][ROW]
370	              acc_S_mn[r, c] = (
371	                -Float32.inf if row_idx < row_limit_top
372	                or row_idx > row_limit_bot else acc_S_mn[r, c]
373	              )

18	@dataclass
19	class Softmax(ParamsBase):
20	  scale_log2: Float32
21	  num_rows: cutlass.Constexpr[int]
22	  row_max: cute.Tensor
23	  row_sum: cute.Tensor
24	  arch: cutlass.Constexpr[int] = 80
25	  softmax_scale: Float32 | None = None
26	
27	  @staticmethod
28	  def create(
29	    scale_log2: Float32,
30	    num_rows: cutlass.Constexpr[int],
31	    arch: cutlass.Constexpr[int] = 80,
32	    softmax_scale: Float32 | None = None,
33	  ):
34	    row_max = cute.make_rmem_tensor(num_rows, Float32)
35	    row_sum = cute.make_rmem_tensor(num_rows, Float32)
36	    return Softmax(scale_log2, num_rows, row_max, row_sum, arch, softmax_scale)
37	
38	  def reset(self) -> None:
39	    self.row_max.fill(-Float32.inf)
40	    self.row_sum.fill(0.0)
41	
42	  def _compute_row_max(
43	    self,
44	    acc_S_row: cute.TensorSSA,
45	    init_val: float | Float32 | None = None
46	  ) -> Float32:
47	    return fmax_reduce(acc_S_row, init_val, arch=self.arch)
48	
49	  def _compute_row_sum(
50	    self,
51	    acc_S_row_exp: cute.TensorSSA,
52	    init_val: float | Float32 | None = None
53	  ) -> Float32:
54	    return fadd_reduce(acc_S_row_exp, init_val, arch=self.arch)
55	
56	  @cute.jit
57	  def online_softmax(
58	    self,
59	    acc_S: cute.Tensor,
60	    is_first: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
61	    check_inf: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = True,
62	  ) -> cute.Tensor:
63	    """Apply online softmax and return the row_scale to rescale O.
64	
65	        :param acc_S: acc_S tensor
66	        :type acc_S: cute.Tensor
67	        :param is_first: is first n_block
68	        :type is_first: cutlass.Constexpr
69	        """
70	    # Change acc_S to M,N layout view.
71	    acc_S_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(acc_S)
72	    row_scale = cute.make_fragment_like(self.row_max, Float32)
73	
74	    row_max = self.row_max
75	    row_sum = self.row_sum
76	    scale_log2 = self.scale_log2
77	    arch = self.arch
78	
79	    # Each iteration processes one row of acc_S
80	    for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(row_max), unroll_full=True):
81	      acc_S_row = acc_S_mn[r, None].load()  # (n_block_size)
82	
83	      row_max_cur = fmax_reduce(
84	        acc_S_row,
85	        init_val=row_max[r] if cutlass.const_expr(not is_first) else None,
86	        arch=arch,
87	      )
88	
89	      row_max_cur = cute.arch.warp_reduction_max(
90	        row_max_cur, threads_in_group=4
91	      )
92	      # Update row_max before changing row_max_cur to safe value for -inf
93	      row_max_prev = row_max[r]
94	      row_max[r] = row_max_cur
95	
96	      if cutlass.const_expr(check_inf):  # apply mask
97	        row_max_cur = 0.0 if row_max_cur == -Float32.inf else row_max_cur
98	
99	      if cutlass.const_expr(is_first):
100	        row_max_cur_scaled = row_max_cur * scale_log2
101	        acc_S_row_exp = cute.math.exp2(
102	          acc_S_row * scale_log2 - row_max_cur_scaled, fastmath=True
103	        )
104	        acc_S_row_sum = fadd_reduce(acc_S_row_exp, init_val=None, arch=arch)
105	        row_scale[r] = 1.0
106	      else:
107	        row_max_cur_scaled = row_max_cur * scale_log2
108	        acc_S_row_exp = cute.math.exp2(
109	          acc_S_row * scale_log2 - row_max_cur_scaled, fastmath=True
110	        )
111	        # row_scale[r] = cute.math.exp2(row_max_prev * self.scale_log2 - row_max_cur_scaled)
112	        row_scale[r] = cute.math.exp2((row_max_prev - row_max_cur) * scale_log2,
113	                                      fastmath=True)
114	        acc_S_row_sum = fadd_reduce(
115	          acc_S_row_exp, init_val=row_sum[r] * row_scale[r], arch=arch
116	        )
117	
118	      row_sum[r] = acc_S_row_sum
119	      acc_S_mn[r, None].store(acc_S_row_exp)
120	
121	    return row_scale
122	
123	  @cute.jit
124	  def finalize(
125	    self,
126	    final_scale: Float32 = 1.0,
127	    sink_val: Float32 | cute.Tensor | None = None
128	  ) -> cute.Tensor:
129	    """Finalize the online softmax by computing the scale and logsumexp."""
130	    if cutlass.const_expr(
131	      sink_val is not None and isinstance(sink_val, cute.Tensor)
132	    ):
133	      assert cute.size(sink_val) == cute.size(self.row_sum)
134	    row_sum = self.row_sum
135	    row_max = self.row_max
136	    scale_log2 = self.scale_log2
137	
138	    # quad reduction for row_sum as we didn't do it during each iteration of online softmax
139	    row_sum.store(warp_reduce(row_sum.load(), operator.add, width=4))
140	    row_scale = cute.make_fragment_like(row_max, Float32)
141	
142	    for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(row_sum), unroll_full=True):
143	      if cutlass.const_expr(sink_val is not None):
144	        sink_val_cur = sink_val if not isinstance(sink_val,
145	                                                  cute.Tensor) else sink_val[r]
146	        LOG2_E = math.log2(math.e)
147	        row_sum[r] += cute.math.exp2(
148	          sink_val_cur * LOG2_E - row_max[r] * scale_log2, fastmath=True
149	        )
150	
151	      # if row_sum is zero or nan, set acc_O_mn_row to 1.0
152	      acc_O_mn_row_is_zero_or_nan = row_sum[r] == 0.0 or row_sum[r] != row_sum[r]
153	      row_scale[r] = (
154	        cute.arch.
155	        rcp_approx(row_sum[r] if not acc_O_mn_row_is_zero_or_nan else 1.0)
156	      ) * final_scale
157	      row_sum_cur = row_sum[r]
158	      LN2 = math.log(2.0)
159	      row_sum[r] = (
160	        (row_max[r] * scale_log2 + cute.math.log2(row_sum_cur, fastmath=True)) *
161	        LN2 if not acc_O_mn_row_is_zero_or_nan else -Float32.inf
162	      )
163	    return row_scale
164	
165	  @cute.jit
166	  def rescale_O(self, acc_O: cute.Tensor, row_scale: cute.Tensor) -> None:
167	    """Scale each row of acc_O by the given scale tensor.
168	        :param acc_O: input tensor
169	        :type acc_O: cute.Tensor
170	        :param row_scale: row_scale tensor
171	        :type row_scale: cute.Tensor
172	        """
173	    acc_O_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(acc_O)
174	    assert cute.size(row_scale) == cute.size(acc_O_mn, mode=[0])
175	    for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(row_scale), unroll_full=True):
176	      acc_O_mn[r, None].store(acc_O_mn[r, None].load() * row_scale[r])

179	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
180	# V2 (2-WG asymmetric PV) cross-WG row-scale handoff helpers
181	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
182	# WG1 owns softmax and computes row_scale; WG2 must apply the same row_scale
183	# to its acc_O_back. We hand it off through `sScale` shaped (tile_m, num_stages_p).
184	#
185	# Layout convention: tiled_mma_qk and tiled_mma_pv both use atom_M=64 (single
186	# WGMMA M-mode atom), so per-thread M-partition is identical between them. Each
187	# row of M is owned by a 4-thread quad: only `lane % 4 == 0` writes; all 4
188	# threads in the quad read (and within each WG, all threads with that row
189	# coordinate read).
190	
191	
192	@cute.jit
193	def store_scale_to_smem(
194	  row_scale: cute.Tensor,
195	  sScale: cute.Tensor,
196	  stage,
197	  tiled_mma: cute.TiledMma,
198	  tidx,
199	  tile_m: cutlass.Constexpr[int],
200	  tile_n: cutlass.Constexpr[int],
201	):
202	  """WG1 writes its per-thread row_scale fragment into sScale[row_global, stage].
203	
204	    Only the leftmost-quad thread (col offset 0) of each row writes. Per-thread
205	    fragment length must equal the WGMMA C-frag's num_rows_per_thread (typically 2
206	    for atom_M=64).
207	    """
208	  thr_mma = tiled_mma.get_slice(tidx)
209	  cS = cute.make_identity_tensor((tile_m, tile_n))
210	  tScS = thr_mma.partition_C(cS)
211	  tScS_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(tScS)
212	  # tScS_mn[r, c] = (row_global, col_global) tuple
213	  if tScS_mn[0, 0][1] == 0:
214	    for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(row_scale), unroll_full=True):
215	      r_global = tScS_mn[r, 0][0]
216	      sScale[r_global, stage] = row_scale[r]
217	
218	
219	@cute.jit
220	def load_scale_from_smem(
221	  sScale: cute.Tensor,
222	  stage,
223	  tiled_mma: cute.TiledMma,
224	  tidx,
225	  tile_m: cutlass.Constexpr[int],
226	  tile_n: cutlass.Constexpr[int],
227	  num_rows: cutlass.Constexpr[int],
228	) -> cute.Tensor:
229	  """Each thread loads its owned rows of row_scale from sScale[row_global, stage]."""
230	  row_scale = cute.make_rmem_tensor(num_rows, Float32)
231	  thr_mma = tiled_mma.get_slice(tidx)
232	  cS = cute.make_identity_tensor((tile_m, tile_n))
233	  tScS = thr_mma.partition_C(cS)
234	  tScS_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(tScS)
235	  for r in cutlass.range(num_rows, unroll_full=True):
236	    r_global = tScS_mn[r, 0][0]
237	    row_scale[r] = sScale[r_global, stage]
238	  return row_scale
